title: Laboratory technicians
description: Apply theory and principles of civil engineering in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of structures and facilities under the direction of engineering staff or physical scientists. Apply theory and principles of environmental engineering to modify, test, and operate equipment and devices used in the prevention, control, and remediation of environmental problems, including waste treatment and site remediation, under the direction of engineering staff or scientists. May assist in the development of environmental remediation devices. Apply engineering theory and principles to problems of industrial layout or manufacturing production, usually under the direction of engineering staff. May perform time and motion studies on worker operations in a variety of industries for purposes such as establishing standard production rates or improving efficiency. (Based on 35 related US occupations.)

Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others

tasks.task: Prepare samples or photomicrographs for testing and analysis, including cell samples and biological specimens.

Discuss test results and analyses with customers and provide information or technical assistance to the public or government representatives regarding public health, environmental protection, or workplace safety.
Keep detailed logs of all work-related activities and document and maintain records of testing results or other documents as required by governing agencies.
Record test data and prepare reports, summaries, or charts that interpret test results, including compiling materials for distribution to pathologists.

Collect samples of gases, soils, water, industrial wastewater, or asbestos products to conduct tests on pollutant levels and investigate hazardous conditions or spills, and monitor emission control devices for compliance with regulations.
Calibrate microscopes or test instruments and set up, adjust, maintain, calibrate, clean, and troubleshoot laboratory and field equipment.
Calculate the amount of pollutants in samples or compute air pollution or gas flow in industrial processes using chemical and mathematical formulas.
Make recommendations to control or eliminate unsafe conditions at workplaces or public facilities and initiate procedures to address violations of environmental or health regulations.
Maintain files, such as hazardous waste databases, chemical usage data, personnel exposure information, or diagrams showing equipment locations.
Isolate, identify, and prepare specimens for examination, including examining specimens for presence of contaminants or abnormalities using microscopes and other methods.
Perform statistical analysis of environmental data and analyze experimental data to interpret results and write reports and summaries of findings.
Set up equipment or stations to monitor and collect pollutants and supervise laboratory activities or other workers in laboratory settings.
Monitor laboratory work to ensure compliance with set standards and develop or implement programs for monitoring of environmental pollution or quality assurance to ensure accurate test performance.
Input data into databases and input details of specimen processing, analysis, and technical issues into logs or laboratory information systems.
Use computers, computer-interfaced equipment, or robotics to perform work duties, including advancing precision agriculture technology and geospatial analysis for agricultural applications.
Conduct research and collect information and samples to assist in laboratory work, including monitoring effectiveness of integrated pest management practices or agricultural applications.
Prepare laboratory samples for analysis and ensure compliance with proper protocols in sampling and disposal.
Provide technical support and services for scientists and engineers, and develop or implement training programs for laboratory personnel and trainees.
Analyze test results to classify products, examine or evaluate specimen quality, and investigate treatment effectiveness for agricultural and environmental practices.
Maintain effective laboratory operations by adhering to standards of specimen collection, preparation or laboratory safety and performing regular maintenance of laboratory equipment.
Compile and maintain records of preventive maintenance and instrument performance checks according to regulations.
Prepare reports in graphical or tabular form summarizing evaluation outcomes, including field productivity or profitability summaries.
Conduct chemical analysis of body fluids to determine the presence of normal or abnormal components and analyze findings to check accuracy of results.
Provide assistance to food scientists or technologists in research and development or quality control, and offer advice on the development or application of technology for environmental practices.
Identify tissue structures or cell components to be used in the diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of diseases, and analyze samples of biological material for chemical content or reaction.

Operate computerized laboratory equipment to dehydrate, decalcify, or microincinerate tissue samples, and stain tissue specimens with dyes or other chemicals to make cell details visible under microscopes.

Perform procedures associated with histochemistry to prepare specimens for immunofluorescence or microscopy, and prepare or use prepared tissue specimens for teaching, research, or diagnostic purposes.

Prepare substances, such as reagents and dilution, and stains for histological specimens according to protocols, and prepare standard volumetric solutions or reagents to be combined with samples, following standardized formulas or experimental procedures.
Perform tests by following physician instructions and enter data from analysis of medical tests or clinical results into computer for storage.

Analyze the results of tests or experiments to ensure conformity to specifications, using special mechanical or electrical devices, and compile, log, or record testing or operational data for review and further analysis.

Resolve problems with laboratory equipment and instruments, such as microscopes, mass spectrometers, microtomes, immunostainers, tissue processors, embedding centers, and water baths, and maintain or repair laboratory equipment to ensure operational readiness, safety, and cleanliness.

Collect blood or tissue samples from patients, observing principles of asepsis, and conduct chemical analyses of body fluids, such as blood or urine, to detect abnormalities or diseases and enter findings into computer.
Supervise or instruct other technicians or laboratory assistants, and teach students or other staff in laboratory procedures.

Calculate and report results of tests and analyses using specialized software and prepare data summaries, reports, or analyses that include results, charts, or graphs to document research findings and results.
Monitor equipment during operation to ensure adherence to specifications for characteristics such as pressure, temperature, or flow.
Prepare and package environmental samples for shipping or testing, and archive diagnostic material, such as histologic slides and blocks.
Set up, clean, and maintain laboratory equipment, and provide technical support or assistance to chemists or engineers.

Conduct chemical or physical laboratory tests to assist scientists in making qualitative or quantitative analyses of solids, liquids, or gaseous materials, using techniques such as chromatography, spectroscopy, physical or chemical separation techniques, or microscopy.
Train new employees on topics such as the proper operation of laboratory equipment and maintain accurate record or batch-record documentation of nanoproduction.
Prepare detailed verbal or written presentations for scientists, engineers, project managers, or upper management based on laboratory findings or analyses.

Collect or prepare solid or fluid samples for analysis, and participate in geological, geophysical, geochemical, hydrographic, or oceanographic surveys, prospecting field trips, exploratory drilling, well logging, or underground mine survey programs.
Inspect or measure thin films of carbon nanotubes, polymers, or inorganic coatings using a variety of techniques or analytical tools.
Contribute written material or data for grant or patent applications.

Monitor and adjust production processes or equipment for quality and productivity; identify opportunities for improvements in quality, cost, or efficiency of automation equipment; and develop sustainable manufacturing technologies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and waste.
Develop production, inventory, or quality assurance programs, and implement programs to address problems related to production, materials, safety, or quality.

Follow policies and procedures for radiation workers to ensure personnel safety; monitor personnel to determine the amounts and intensities of radiation exposure; and educate personnel about radiation safety procedures.

Modify, devise, or maintain nuclear equipment used in operations; test plant equipment to ensure proper operation; and monitor nuclear reactor equipment performance to identify operational inefficiencies, hazards, or maintenance needs.
Analyze samples, such as air, water, or environmental samples, for contaminants or other elements, and collect and analyze pollution samples to assess levels of contamination.

Record laboratory or field data, including numerical data, test results, and summaries of observations; maintain project logbook records; and prepare reports detailing findings and investigative methods.
Apply safety tags to equipment needing maintenance, and plan, design, and improve components of environmental monitoring systems.
Perform environmental quality work in field or office settings, and review technical documents to ensure completeness and conformance to requirements.
Conduct validations or transfers of analytical methods and monitor testing procedures to ensure that tests are performed according to established specifications.
Collect geospatial data using technologies such as aerial photography, light detection systems, or satellites, and integrate remotely sensed data with other geospatial data.
Interpret test results, compare them to specifications, and make recommendations on the appropriateness of data for release, and investigate or report questionable test results.
Identify and troubleshoot equipment problems and verify integrity and accuracy of data contained in remote sensing image analysis systems.
Prepare documentation or presentations, including charts and graphs, to summarize findings and communicate results effectively.
Develop or maintain geospatial information databases to support environmental assessments or research.

Gather and compile data or provide information related to environmental issues; collaborate with agricultural workers to apply remote sensing information to reduce negative environmental impacts; and assist engineers in developing, building, or testing prototypes.
Conduct statistical studies to analyze production costs for sustainable designs.
Follow environmental compliance regulations, and obtain product information or order materials needed for projects.
Prepare solutions, reagents, or sample formulations needed for laboratory work and operate and maintain laboratory equipment and apparatus.
Prepare or review required technical reports containing information collected during analyses.
Receive and inspect raw materials, and ensure that lab cleanliness and safety standards are maintained.
Supply quality control data necessary for regulatory submissions and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
Write or revise standard quality control operating procedures, and develop and qualify new testing methods.
Coordinate testing with contract laboratories and vendors, and review data from contract laboratories for accuracy.
Participate in internal assessments and audits, and complete documentation needed to support testing procedures.
Conduct routine and non-routine analyses of various samples, and compile laboratory test data for analysis.
Calibrate, validate, or maintain laboratory equipment, and evaluate analytical methods to identify improvements.
Train other analysts in laboratory procedures and assays, and serve as a technical liaison between quality control and other departments.
Identify quality problems and recommend solutions, as well as conduct visual inspections of finished products.
Participate in the planning or development of mapping projects and analyze aerial photographs for geographical information.
Research and combine existing property information to describe boundaries, including calculating geographical measurements.
Inspect and monitor construction sites to ensure adherence to safety standards, building codes, or specifications.
Prepare reports and document project activities and data, including comparing survey computations to applicable standards.
Inspect buildings for hazardous conditions and fire code violations, ensuring compliance with laws and safety regulations.
Conduct inspections using survey instruments and monitoring environmental regulations during construction activities.
Compile information necessary for project planning, and recommend energy-efficient technologies or practices.
Conduct tests and analyses to ensure the quality and safety of fire protection systems and evaluate system installations.
Conduct environmental hazard inspections to identify potential issues, and evaluate project details for adherence to environmental regulations.
Organize and maintain records related to surveys, inspections, and compliance with safety and quality standards.
Perform maintenance and minor repairs on firefighting equipment, including vehicles, and write and submit proposals to modify, replace, and repair equipment.
Maintain fire suppression equipment in good condition, checking equipment periodically to ensure that it is ready for use.
Monitor fire suppression expenditures to ensure that they are necessary and reasonable.
Evaluate the performance of assigned firefighting personnel and recommend personnel actions related to disciplinary procedures, performance, leaves of absence, and grievances.

Attend training classes to maintain current knowledge of fire prevention, safety, and firefighting procedures, and instruct and drill fire department personnel in assigned duties, including firefighting, medical care, hazardous materials response, fire prevention, and related subjects.

Inspect stations, uniforms, equipment, or recreation areas to ensure compliance with safety standards, taking corrective action as necessary, and supervise and participate in the inspection of properties to ensure compliance with applicable fire codes, ordinances, laws, regulations, and standards.
Participate in creating fire safety guidelines and evacuation schemes for nonresidential buildings.
Direct the training of firefighters and provide supervisors with reports on training progress and status.
Inspect and test new and existing fire protection systems, fire detection systems, and fire safety equipment to ensure proper operation.
Assess the nature and extent of fire, condition of buildings, danger to adjacent buildings, and water supply status to determine crew or company requirements.

Drive crew carriers to transport firefighters to fire sites, and assign firefighters to jobs at strategic locations to facilitate rescue of persons and maximize the application of extinguishing agents.
Provide emergency medical services as required, and perform light to heavy rescue functions at emergencies.
Document non-destructive testing methods, processes, or results and prepare reports on non-destructive testing results.
Select, calibrate, or operate equipment used in the non-destructive testing of products or materials, and conduct non-destructive testing to identify defects in materials or equipment.
Maintain clean working environments according to clean room standards, and monitor inventory levels and order supplies as necessary.
Examine structures or vehicles using non-destructive testing techniques to identify defects.
Visually examine materials, structures, or components for signs of corrosion, metal fatigue, cracks, or other flaws.
Map the presence of imperfections within objects using sonic measurements.
Document procedures for calibration of optical or fiber optic equipment and prepare reports on test data.

Computer assisted tomography CAT inspection equipment
